# GeoSpoof

> Free, open-source browser extension that spoofs your geolocation, timezone, and WebRTC APIs so websites see a location you choose instead of your real one. Works on Firefox, Chrome, Brave, Edge, and Safari.

GeoSpoof overrides browser location APIs at the page level. It does not change your IP address and is not a VPN; it complements one. The site includes a free in-browser location test and guides on location privacy.

## Pages

- [GeoSpoof — Spoof your browser location & timezone](https://geospoof.com/): Free, open-source browser extension that overrides geolocation, timezone, and WebRTC APIs. Works on Firefox, Chrome, Brave, Edge, and Safari.
- [Browser Location Test](https://geospoof.com/verify): Free tool that shows the geolocation, timezone, and IP address websites can read about you right now, and flags real-vs-reported location leaks.
- [GeoSpoof Blog](https://geospoof.com/blog): Guides on browser location spoofing, timezone privacy, and WebRTC leaks.

## Blog

- [How Websites Actually Know Where You Are](https://geospoof.com/blog/how-websites-know-your-location): Websites pin your location through at least eight independent signals, not just your IP. Here's the full stack, from Wi-Fi positioning to WebRTC, and why a VPN only fixes one of them.
